papers.title_en: Adaptive Space-efficient Collectives for Dynamic and Unstructured Sparsity on GPU Platforms
papers.authors: Lannie Dalton Hough, Emir Gencer, Hoffmann Muki, Abhinav Bhatele
papers.evidence: 利用稀疏性的集合通信算法减少通信量，提升训练效率
papers.tldr: 现有GPU集体通信库NCCL仅优化稠密数据，处理稀疏数据时因格式转换和密化开销导致性能低下。本文提出基于位向量的Pici格式，实现低开销压缩与解压，并设计自适应算法根据稀疏度动态调整数据表示。在99%输入稀疏度下，all-gather、reduce-scatter、all-reduce分别获得最高5.25x、2.5x、2.66x加速，显著提升动态非结构化稀疏场景的通信效率。
papers.abstract_en: High-performance collective communication primitives are necessary for a variety of high performance computing (HPC) and machine learning (ML) workloads. State-of-the-art collective communication libraries such as NCCL optimize exclusively for dense data. However, when sending sparse data, we can reduce communication volume by not sending zeros. Unfortunately, explicitly handling sparsity introduces challenges such as format conversion overheads and densification during collectives that involve reductions. In this paper, we introduce sparsity-exploiting algorithms for three collectives that address these challenges: all-gather, reduce-scatter, and all-reduce. Our collective implementations are backed by a new bitvector-based format, Pici, designed for low overhead and fast (de)compression at moderate sparsities. Further, our algorithms adapt to the level of sparsity in data, modifying its representation during the course of the collective. At 99% input sparsity, our collectives achieve up to 5.25x, 2.5x, and 2.66x speedups over NCCL for all-gather, reduce-scatter, and all-reduce, respectively.
